name: Inflammatory Bowel Disease (IBD)
description: Canine IBD encompasses a group of chronic idiopathic gastrointestinal disorders characterised by persistent gastrointestinal signs and histological evidence of intestinal inflammation. Protein-losing enteropathy is a severe complication.
treatment_overview: Dietary trials with hydrolysed or novel protein diets form the first step. Immunosuppressive therapy (prednisolone ± azathioprine, cyclosporine) is used when diet alone is insufficient. Cobalamin supplementation is often needed.
prevention_tips: Healthy gut microbiome through varied diet and avoiding unnecessary antibiotics
